1115	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
1116	  \begin{enumerate}
1117	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
1118	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
1119	      which is flat over \(T\);

1121	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
1122	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);

1124	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
1125	      \(\Phi\).
1126	  \end{enumerate}

1129	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
1130	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
1131	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
1132	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
1133	\end{definition}

1177	  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
1178	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
1179	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
1180	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
1181	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
1182	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
1183	  \[
1184	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
1185	    \qquad
1186	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
1187	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
1188	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
1189	  \]
1190	  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\); the rank-\(d\) local freeness of
1191	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is as in \cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}. Concretely,
1192	  \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\): the Quot functor
1193	  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) in the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
1194	  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\).
1195	\end{definition}

1266	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
1267	  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of
1268	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
1269	  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
1270	  equipped with a tautological quotient
1271	  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
1272	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
1273	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
1274	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
1275	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)\) -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
1276	
1277	\end{theorem}

1279	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
1280	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
1281	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
1282	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
1283	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
1284	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
1285	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
1286	\begin{proof}
1287	  
1288	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
1289	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
1290	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
1291	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
1292	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
1293	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
1294	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
1295	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
1296	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
1297	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
1298	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
1299	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
1300	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
1301	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
1302	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
1303	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
1304	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
1305	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
1306	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
1307	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
1308	
1309	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
1310	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
1311	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
1312	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
1313	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
1314	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
1315	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
1316	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
1317	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
1318	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
1319	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
1320	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
1321	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
1322	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
1323	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
1324	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
1325	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
1326	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
1327	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
1328	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
1329	\end{proof}

1331	\section{Out of scope}
1332	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
1333	
1334	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
1335	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
1336	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
1337	\begin{itemize}
1338	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
1339	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
1340	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
1341	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
1342	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
1343	    associated prerequisites -- not constructed in this chapter.
1344	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
1345	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
1346	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
1347	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
1348	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
1349	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
1350	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
1351	    Lean formalization is a prerequisite sub-task.
1352	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
1353	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
1354	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
1355	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
1356	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream. Its blueprint home is the
1357	    FGA Picard-representability assembly (the chapter that bundles the relative
1358	    Picard functor, its representability, and the abelian-group-scheme
1359	    structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)); that assembly lives in the parent project
1360	    which consumes this Quot-foundations layer and is not part of the present
1361	    subproject.
1362	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
1363	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
1364	    constructed in this chapter.
1365	\end{itemize}

1175	  Here the two functors \((g')^*\) and \(f'_*\) are taken along the pullback cone legs,
1176	  which are \emph{not} a priori \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-maps; this is where
1177	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor} enters. By that lemma
1178	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}, the first projection \(g'\) is the
1179	  \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map of the inclusion \(A \to R' \otimes_R A\), and the second
1180	  projection \(f'\) is the \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map of \(R' \to R' \otimes_R A\).
1181	  With the legs so identified the affine dictionaries apply:
1182	  the pullback dictionary Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} along
1183	  \(A \to R' \otimes_R A\) reads \((g')^*\widetilde M\) as the extension of scalars
1184	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\), and the pushforward dictionary
1185	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} along \(R' \to R' \otimes_R A\) reads
1186	  \(f'_*\) of it as restriction of scalars back to \(R'\). The result is
1187	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) as an \(R'\)-module, and composing the two dictionary
1188	  isomorphisms is \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\). This matches the Stacks description
1189	  \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\), with \(\mathcal{F}'\) the tilde of
1190	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\).
1191	\end{proof}

1193	% --- Supporting lemmas for the codomain read ---
1194	% These helpers establish that pullback along a scheme isomorphism is an equivalence
1195	% of module categories; the adjunction unit of an equivalence is an isomorphism,
1196	% used in the codomain-read computation.
1197	
1198	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1199	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1200	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1201	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1202	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1203	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1204	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1205	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1206	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1207	\end{lemma}
1208	\begin{proof}
1209	
1210	\leanok
1211	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1212	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1213	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1214	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1215	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1216	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1217	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1218	\end{proof}
1219	
1220	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1221	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1222	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1223	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1224	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1225	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1226	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1227	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1228	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1229	\end{lemma}
1230	\begin{proof}
1231	
1232	\leanok
1233	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1234	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1235	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1236	\end{proof}

1242	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1243	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1244	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1245	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1246	  % NOTE: tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1247	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1248	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1249	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1250	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1251	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1252	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1253	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1254	  % L933--938).
1255	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1256	  % equality
1257	  % $$
1258	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1259	  % $$
1260	  % as $R'$-modules."
1261	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1262	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1263	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1264	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1265	  \[
1266	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1267	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1268	  \]
1269	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1270	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1271	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1272	  hypothesis.
1273	\end{lemma}
1274	
1275	\begin{proof}
1276	  \uses{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv, lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1277	  \leanok
1278	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1279	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the standalone geometry-free helper
1280	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_regroup_linearEquiv}, whose construction is now the
1281	  natively \(R'\)-linear pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1282	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1283	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1284	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1285	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1286	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1287	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1288	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1289	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1290	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1291	
1292	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1293	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1294	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1295	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1296	  dictionaries,
1297	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1298	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1299	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1300	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1301	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1302	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1303	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1304	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1305	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1306	  \[
1307	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1308	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1309	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1310	  \]
1311	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1312	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1313	
1314	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1315	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1316	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1317	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1318	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1319	\end{proof}

1943	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
1944	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
1945	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
1946	  \mathlibok
1947	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1948	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
1949	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
1950	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
1951	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
1952	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
1953	  \[
1954	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
1955	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1956	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
1957	  \]
1958	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
1959	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
1960	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
1961	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
1962	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
1963	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
1964	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
1965	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
1966	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
1967	\end{lemma}
